rationale: The plan is implementation-ready and internally consistent against its stated objective, trust model, and its own decision ledger (D1–D30). I verified the load-bearing properties rather than the prose. (1) Trust-model conformance: spine read-only is preserved (Task 0 touches only ci.yml; Task 1 touches only .gitignore outside clotho/; Task 7's diff review blocks spine changes); advisory-only is mechanically proven in both directions (inbound no-import with symlink/real-path and nonliteral-require hardening per D23, outbound closed allowlist over all specifier forms per D27, loader-construction prohibition per D30 with an honest syntactic proof boundary); fail-closed discipline is pervasive and consistent — unknown kinds/statuses/schemes reject, shallow history rejects rather than minting a wrong repository identity (D16/D18 with a real-git integration fixture, not just injected units), and coverage-unknown replaces silently-smaller answers (D11). (2) Internal coherence of the hardest sequencing constraints: D17 (inventories name only existing files) is threaded correctly through Tasks 4a/4b/5, and D19 cleanly splits Task 3 generic ledger validation (injected fixture coverage) from Task 5 driver-level inventory-equality enforcement — this resolves what would otherwise be a circular dependency between committed inventories and ledger validation. Task ordering (scaffold before workflow matrix entry) avoids a knowingly-red workflow PR, and the plan explains why 'Task 0' executes after Task 1. (3) Accounting soundness: D5/D24/D26/D29 form a closed loop — weavers return only {edges, warnings}, counts are driver-owned via counted iterators, 'executed' requires exhaustion plus cardinality equality, the normative edge-append ordering prevents an incomplete weaver from influencing the temporary ledger, and the verifier's proof boundary is honestly scoped (it does not claim to reconstruct runtime exhaustion from the signed ledger). (4) Publication semantics: D20/D21/D22/D28 give a coherent atomic no-replace pipeline with a frozen commit point, physical (not lexical) containment checked twice, idempotent abort() on every failure path, and three machine-visible publication states; the residual symlink race and cleanup-incomplete residue are explicitly bounded as accepted risks on an advisory, git-ignored artifact. (5) Exit criteria are concrete and testable: flagship eight-group hand-audited expected set with one-to-one matching and a mandatory unscored review set (D3), a skipped-weaver negative run asserting coverage-unknown rather than missing-edge, a 120s runtime ceiling, and reproduction evidence verified from committed bytes. (6) Data model: locator schemas, endpoint matrix (including the spec v2.3 discharges rows and supersedes direction), status/assertor coupling with human-only adjudication, and the single named commit exception to repository-scoping are all specified exactly once and cross-referenced consistently; I found no contradiction between the decisions table, the global constraints, the normative data model, and the per-task checklists. Residual risks (rename discontinuity, over-threading, version-node churn, shared-history namespace, abort-on-failure availability) are explicitly chosen, bounded, and appropriate for an advisory Phase 1 artifact. Minor observations that do not block: the registry test file also hosts the real-git fixture (a cosmetic file-placement choice), and the workflow-only Task 0 relies on human review per the documented governance path — both are within the plan's stated conventions. I would stake this seat's signature on executing this plan as written.
